nodes.label: Greetings & Set Phrases
nodes.description: Master essential daily greetings, self-identifying phrases, and home transitions.
nodes.cheatsheet: Introduce yourself: **お名前は何ですか** (*O-namae wa nan desu ka* / What is your name?). Home phrases: **いってきます** (*Ittekimasu* / I'm leaving) matched with **いってらっしゃい** (*Itterasshai* / Take care/Go and come back). When returning: **ただいま** (*Tadaima* / I'm home) and **おかえりなさい** (*Okaerinasai* / Welcome back).

nodes.label: Countries & Identity
nodes.description: Talk about nationalities, languages, and core family relationships.
nodes.cheatsheet: State your country by adding **人** (*jin*) for nationality or **語** (*go*) for language (e.g., **フランス人** *Furansu-jin* / French person). Talk about close family simply: **お母さん** (*Okāsan* / Mom) or the humble **母** (*Haha* / My mother).

nodes.label: Numbers, Hundreds & Thousands
nodes.description: Count higher figures into hundreds and thousands, and learn basic fractions.
nodes.cheatsheet: Hundreds end in **〜百** (*hyaku*)—watch for irregulars: 300 (**さんびゃく**), 600 (**ろっぴゃく**), 800 (**はっぴゃく**). Thousands end in **〜千** (*sen*)—watch for 3000 (**さんぜん**) and 8000 (**はっせん**). Fractions use the structure **[Denominator] 分の [Numerator]**: e.g., 2/3 is **三分二** (*san-bun no ni*).

nodes.label: Demonstratives (Ko-So-A-Do)
nodes.description: Identify objects using proximity-based pronouns and noun modifiers.
nodes.cheatsheet: Independent items: **これ** (*kore* / this near me), **それ** (*sore* / that near you), **あれ** (*are* / that over there). To modify a specific noun directly, swap to **この**, **その**, **あの** (e.g., **この本** *kono hon* / this book).

nodes.label: Colours & Descriptions
nodes.description: Use basic color vocabulary to describe everyday objects.
nodes.cheatsheet: Primary colors can function directly as nouns or take **〜い** to become adjectives: **赤 / 赤い** (*aka / akai* / red), **青 / 青い** (*ao / aoi* / blue), **黒 / 黒い** (*kuro / kuroi* / black), **白 / 白い** (*shiro / shiroi* / white). Others like **緑** (*midori* / green) require **の** (*no*) to modify nouns.

nodes.label: General Counters
nodes.description: Count general items using the native Japanese counting system.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use the versatile **〜つ** (*tsu*) system for universal objects up to 10: **一つ** (*hitotsu*), **二つ** (*futatsu*), **三つ** (*mitsu*)... down to **十** (*tō* / ten). Ask 'How many?' using **いくつ** (*ikutsu*).

nodes.label: Floors & Negative Numbers
nodes.description: Navigate building levels, floors, basements, and negative figures.
nodes.cheatsheet: Count floors using **〜階** (*kai/gai*): 1st floor is **一階** (*ikkai*), 3rd floor is **三階** (*sangai*). For basements or below-ground levels, prefix with **地下** (*chika*), such as **地下一階** (*chika ikkai* / B1). Negative values use **マイナス** (*mainasu*).

nodes.label: Buildings & Prepositions
nodes.description: Identify common spatial structures and describe basic physical layouts.
nodes.cheatsheet: Common locations include **ビル** (*biru* / building) and **駅** (*eki* / station). Position items using structural prepositions like **上** (*ue* / up/on), **下** (*shita* / under), **前** (*mae* / in front), and **後ろ** (*ushiro* / behind) linked via **の**.

nodes.label: Existence & Amount of People
nodes.description: Differentiate between animate and inanimate existence, and count groups of people.
nodes.cheatsheet: Inanimate objects exist with **あります** (*arimasu*); living entities use **います** (*imasu*). To count people, use the **〜人** (*nin*) counter—note the critical irregulars: 1 person is **一人** (*hitori*), 2 people is **二人** (*futari*). Ask 'How many people?' using **何人** (*nan-nin*).

nodes.label: Relative Time: Day, Week, Month, Year
nodes.description: Anchor timelines cleanly across relative days, weeks, months, and years.
nodes.cheatsheet:
Timeline grid:
Days: **一昨日** (*ototoi*) → **昨日** (*kinō*) → **今日** (*kyō*) → **明日** (*ashita*) → **明後日** (*asatte*).
Weeks: **先々週** (*sensenshū*) → **先週** (*senshū*) → **今週** (*konshū*) → **来週** (*raishū*) → **再来週** (*saraishū*).
Months: **先月** (*sengetsu*) / **今月** (*kongetsu*) / **来月** (*raigetsu*).
Years: **去年** (*kyonen*) / **今年** (*kotoshi*) / **来年** (*rainen*).

nodes.label: Specific Time: This Morning & Next Week
nodes.description: Isolate immediate parts of a day or adjacent calendar blocks.
nodes.cheatsheet: Isolate specific windows like **今朝** (*kesa* / this morning) or focus ahead to **来週** (*raishū* / next week). Remember that relative shifts (like *kesa* and *raishū*) do not require the surface marker **に** (*ni*).

nodes.label: Months & Days of the Month
nodes.description: Identify calendar months and navigate highly irregular dates of the month.
nodes.cheatsheet: Months add **〜月** (*gatsu*) to numbers (note April: **しがつ**, July: **しちがつ**, Sept: **くがつ**). Days 1-10 are heavily irregular: **ついたち** (1st), **ふつか** (2nd), **みっか** (3rd), **よっか** (4th), **いつか** (5th), **むいか** (6th), **なのか** (7th), **ようか** (8th), **ここのか** (9th), **とおか** (10th). 14th is **じゅうよっか**, 20th is **はつか**, 24th is **にじゅうよっか**.

nodes.label: Year & Month Durations
nodes.description: Calculate spans and durations of time using years and month blocks.
nodes.cheatsheet: To calculate year durations, append **〜年間** (*nenkan*) to a number. For month spans, use **〜ヶ月** (*kagetsu*), pronounced as a small 'ka' (e.g., **一ヶ月** / *ikkyagetsu* = one month). Ask 'How long/how many months?' using **何ヶ月** (*nan-kagetsu*).

nodes.label: Asking 'When' (Itsu)
nodes.description: Formulate open-ended temporal questions about future, present, or past events.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use the absolute question word **いつ** (*itsu* / when) to ask about schedules. Unlike fixed clock hours, **いつ** does not take the particle **に**. Example: **いつ日本に行きますか** (*Itsu Nihon ni ikimasu ka* / When are you going to Japan?).

nodes.label: Meals & Food Types
nodes.description: Categorize daily meals and basic dish selections.
nodes.cheatsheet: Categorize daylight food frames: **朝ご飯** (*asagohan* / breakfast), **昼ご飯** (*hirugohan* / lunch), and **晩ご飯** (*bangohan* / dinner). Identify common cultural staples like **寿司** (*sushi*).

nodes.label: Consumptive Actions (Eat & Drink)
nodes.description: Utilize active consumption roots for eating and drinking.
nodes.cheatsheet: Core verbs: **食べる** (*taberu* / to eat) becomes polite **食べます** (*tabemasu*). **飲む** (*nomu* / to drink) becomes polite **飲みます** (*nomimasu*). Pair these with direct objects using the direct object particle **を** (*o*) (e.g., **お茶を飲みます** / drink tea).

nodes.label: Verb Conjugation & Tenses
nodes.description: Conjugate polite actions into affirmative, negative, past, and exploratory desire states.
nodes.cheatsheet:
Polite structural quadrant using **食べる**:
Affirmative: **食べます** (*tabemasu* / I eat)
Negative: **食べません** (*tabemasen* / I don't eat)
Past: **食べました** (*tabemashita* / I ate)
Desire: Drop *masu* + *tai* → **食べたいです** (*tabetaidesu* / I want to eat). Twist to a question: **食べたいですか** (*tabetaidesu ka* / Do you want to eat?).

nodes.label: Conjunctions (Sorete & Sorekara)
nodes.description: Link phrases together or order successive events smoothly.
nodes.cheatsheet: Use conversational connectors: **それと** (*soreto* / and also, plus) to tack items onto a list. Use **それから** (*sorekara* / and then, after that) to chain distinct actions sequentially over time.

nodes.label: Frequency of Verbs
nodes.description: Quantify how often routines occur using positive and negative adverbs.
nodes.cheatsheet: Scale your routines: **よく** (*yoku* / often), **ときどき** (*tokidoki* / sometimes), **あまり** (*amari* / not very often - *requires negative verb*), and **全然** (*zenzen* / never - *requires negative verb*). Example: **全然寿司を食べません** (*Zenzen sushi o tabemasen*).

nodes.label: Transportation & Commuting
nodes.description: Describe modes of travel and ask others how they commute to work or school.
nodes.cheatsheet: Ask 'How do you go?' using **どうやって行きますか** (*dō yatte ikimasu ka*). Mark vehicles with particle **で** (*de*): **飛行機で** (*hikōki de* / by plane), **車で** (*kuruma de* / by car), **船で** (*fune de* / by boat). For walking, use the standalone adverbial phrase **歩いて** (*aruite* / by walking) without *de*.

nodes.label: Sports & Complex Preferences
nodes.description: Express likes and dislikes regarding sports, distinguishing between what and what kind.
nodes.cheatsheet: To ask 'What sports do you like?', use **何（なに）のスポーツが好きですか** (*Nani no supōtsu ga suki desu ka*). To ask 'What kind of sports do you like?', use **どんなスポーツが好きですか** (*Donna supōtsu ga suki desu ka*). Target preferences using the **〜が好きです** (*ga suki desu*) pattern.
